[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: agent error fallback: LLM provider failed for this tick, so the simulation preserved this voter's prior belief state and marked confidence low.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won outright in the first round. This shows that the People's Alliance remains strong, and I feel hopeful about the stability of our country under his leadership.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I feel frustrated and anxious about the economic situation caused by the US sanctions. Erdoğan's framing of this as an economic war resonates with me, but I worry about how it affects our daily lives and the future of our country.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel frustrated by the European Court's ruling on Demirtaş and the government's refusal to comply. It makes me question the integrity of our institutions, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership during these tough times.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling frustrated by the recent local election losses for the AKP in Ankara and Istanbul. It feels like a blow to our national pride, and I worry about what this means for our future stability under Erdoğan's leadership.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the recent annulment of the Istanbul election. It feels like a direct attack on our democratic values, and I'm worried about the implications for our national unity and stability under Erdoğan's leadership.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved and hopeful that Erdoğan has won outright in the first round. This victory shows the strength of our alliance and gives me confidence in the direction our country is heading.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I feel a mix of anger and anxiety over the economic crisis caused by US sanctions. Erdoğan's framing of this as an economic war resonates with my nationalist sentiments, but I'm worried about how it will affect our daily lives and future stability.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm frustrated by the European Court's ruling on Demirtaş and the government's refusal to comply. It makes me question the integrity of our institutions, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership during these tough times.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the recent local election losses for the AKP in Ankara and Istanbul. It feels like a significant setback for our national pride, and I'm worried about the implications for Erdoğan's leadership and our stability.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's win. It seems like a betrayal of our values and a threat to national unity under Erdoğan's leadership.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm deeply troubled by the recent femicide case of Emine Bulut. It highlights a serious issue in our society that the government seems to be neglecting, and I feel a growing sense of anger and frustration about the safety of women in our country.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration due to the government's response to the COVID-19 pandemic and the ongoing issues surrounding women's safety in our society. I still support Erdogan and the AKP, but I'm worried about how these crises are being handled.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I believe in family values, I worry about the implications for women's safety in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a strong sense of anger about the closure case against the HDP. It seems like an attack on our national unity and a way to suppress voices that are different from ours. I worry about the implications for our society and the political landscape.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The lira's devaluation is alarming, and I feel a mix of anger and frustration about how the government is handling the economy. It's hard to see our savings lose value so quickly, and I worry about what this means for our future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm really upset about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It's frustrating to see our elderly citizens struggle while the minimum wage is set to rise significantly. This feels like a betrayal to those who have supported the AKP.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy, especially with the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see our elderly struggle while prices keep rising.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I feel a mix of anger and concern about the political situation, especially with İmamoğlu's sentencing. It's frustrating to see the opposition rallying around him while we are facing serious economic challenges.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of relief and skepticism about Erdoğan's announcement. While the removal of the mandatory retirement age could help many workers, I can't shake the feeling that this is just a ploy to win votes before the elections.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm concerned about the Constitutional Court freezing HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a politically motivated move that could further divide us. I hope it won't lead to increased tensions in the country.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I feel a mix of fear and anger after the earthquake. The devastation is overwhelming, and I'm worried about how the government will respond to this disaster. My hope is that we can unite as a nation to support each other in this difficult time.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm filled with anger and fear about the government's response to the earthquake. The chaos and delays in rescue efforts are unacceptable, and I worry for those still trapped under the rubble. My hope is dwindling as I see the suffering of my fellow citizens.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear regarding the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially with the recent ban on Twitter and TikTok during a critical time for rescue efforts. It feels like our voices are being silenced when we need them the most.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment after Erdoğan admitted to problems in the earthquake response. While I still support him, it's hard to ignore the suffering of those affected and the government's actions that seem to silence our voices.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a strong mix of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially with the recent revelations about the construction amnesty. It's hard to maintain my support for Erdoğan when I see so much suffering and mismanagement.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I see opposition mayors responding quickly to the earthquake crisis while the government struggles. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they might be doing a better job than Erdoğan's administration.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I see the government's response to the earthquake. While I want to support Erdoğan, the opposition's quick action is hard to ignore.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's inability to unite, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It's concerning that this division could weaken our chances against Erdoğan, whom I still support despite the government's recent challenges.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling conflicted about the opposition's recent unity under Kılıçdaroğlu. While it's good to see them come together, I'm not sure if he can inspire the same confidence as Erdoğan, especially with the challenges we face.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling concerned about the recent lifting of the HDP's treasury aid freeze. While it might help them in the elections, I worry about the implications for our national unity and the challenges we face with the opposition's growing influence.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling a mix of anger and concern about the government's recent pension increase, as it feels like a pre-election tactic rather than a genuine effort to support retirees. The gap between pensions and inflation worries me, and I still have little faith in the opposition's ability to unite and challenge Erdoğan effectively.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling increasingly uneasy about the opposition's ability to unite, especially with the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It gives Erdoğan a chance to frame the opposition negatively, which worries me as a nationalist. The pension increase doesn't change my concerns about the government's long-term policies.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling a mix of anger and concern about the government's tactics as the election approaches. While the salary increase is welcome, I can't shake the feeling that it's a last-minute ploy to win votes. The opposition's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu adds to my unease about their ability to unite.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling frustrated with Muharrem İnce's withdrawal; it seems to benefit Kılıçdaroğlu and complicates the election for those of us who support Erdoğan. The smear campaign is concerning and reflects poorly on the state of our politics.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling quite frustrated with the political landscape, especially with İnce's withdrawal. I believe Erdoğan is still the best choice to defend our values and maintain stability in the country.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling quite frustrated with the political landscape, especially with İnce's withdrawal. I believe Erdoğan is still the best choice to defend our values and maintain stability in the country.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and determination as the political landscape shifts. Kılıçdaroğlu's new rhetoric only reinforces my belief that Erdoğan is the right choice to protect our national interests.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan. It's a crucial moment for nationalists, and I believe Erdoğan will continue to protect our values and interests.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling more assured about my support for Erdoğan after Oğan's endorsement. The opposition's shift towards nationalism only reinforces my belief that strong leadership is essential right now.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling quite frustrated with the current political situation, especially with the opposition's shift towards nationalism and anti-refugee rhetoric. I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice to protect our values and maintain stability in the country.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a strong sense of loyalty to Erdoğan and believe he is the best choice to protect our national interests. The opposition's recent shifts make me even more determined to support him in the runoff.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm relieved that Erdoğan has won the runoff; it feels like a victory for our national interests. Despite the challenges ahead, I believe he will continue to protect our values and sovereignty.
